title: viola × ogawae f. variegata
intro: Viola × ogawae f. variegata, also known as the Japanese pansy orchid or variegated viola, is a unique and exotic plant species that is a hybrid of Viola × ogawae and another viola species, characterized by its distinctive variegated or multicolored foliage. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating skin conditions and wounds. In terms of health benefits, the plant has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Viola × ogawae f. variegata typically involve infusions of the leaves or roots, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of creams or ointments.
